name: Old Fashioned Spiced Tomato Preserves
recipeInstructions:
1. Scald, peel, and chop tomatoes. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
2. Measure 3 cups of the tomatoes into a 6 or 8 quart saucepan.
3. Add lemon juice, grated lemon rind, allspice, cinnamon, and ground cloves to the tomatoes in the saucepan.
4. Measure sugar and set aside.
5. Stir sure-jell fruit pectin into the prepared tomatoes mixture. Ensure the saucepan is no more than 1/3 full to allow for a rolling boil.
6. Bring the mixture to a full boil over high heat, stirring constantly.
7. At once stir in the sugar. Continue stirring until the mixture reaches a full rolling boil (a boil that cannot be stirred down).
8. Boil hard for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
9. Remove from heat and skim off any foam with a large metal spoon.
10. Ladle into hot sterilized jars, leaving 1/4 inch of space at the top.
11. Wipe jar rims and threads clean with a damp cloth.
12. Immediately cover jars with hot lids and screw bands on firmly.
13. Let stand to cool completely.
14. Check seals.
15. Store jam in a cool dry place. Yield: approximately 5 cups.
